_comment: XMclaw Daemon Configuration — copy to config.json and fill in your API keys

evolution._comment: Epic #4: evolution visibility + auto-apply. auto_apply=false (default) means the orchestrator observes SKILL_CANDIDATE_PROPOSED events but does not mutate HEAD — users opt-in explicitly. Flip to true after you've watched the pipeline for a while and trust the gates.

memory._comment: Epic #5: v2 sqlite-vec memory. db_path defaults to ~/.xmclaw/v2/memory.db. ttl is seconds-per-layer (null disables TTL prune for that layer). pinned_tags are metadata tags whose items are never evicted. retention runs periodic prune+cap sweeps in the daemon; set enabled: false to disable the store entirely.

retention._comment: max_items / max_bytes per layer (null = unbounded). sweep_interval_s throttles the background task. prune_by_ttl triggers age-based prune on every tick.

tools._comment: Optional sandbox + per-family kill-switches. With NO tools section (or this empty shape), the agent gets full user-level filesystem, shell, and web access -- the default local-assistant posture. Add allowed_dirs to restrict filesystem tools to specific paths; set enable_bash / enable_web to false to disable those families.

runtime._comment: Epic #3: which SkillRuntime executes forked skills. 'local' = in-process asyncio task + CPU-timeout; good for dev + conformance tests. 'process' = separate OS process via multiprocessing.spawn; real SIGKILL + import isolation + crash containment; fs/net/memory are still advisory (see providers/runtime/process.py). Default is 'local'; production should set 'process'.

security._comment: Epic #14: prompt-injection policy applied to untrusted tool output before it re-enters the LLM context. Accepts: 'detect_only' (log, pass through), 'redact' (replace matched spans with [redacted:<pattern_id>]), 'block' (abort the turn with ANTI_REQ_VIOLATION). Defaults to 'detect_only'.
security._comment_guardians: Epic #3: pre-execution tool security. enabled=false (default) skips all guardian scans; enabled=true wraps every tool call with FilePathToolGuardian + RuleBasedToolGuardian + ShellEvasionGuardian. The optional 'policy' map controls what happens at each severity: 'allow' passes through, 'approve' creates a pending approval (NEEDS_APPROVAL:<id>), 'deny' blocks immediately. Defaults: critical=deny, high=approve, medium/low/info=allow. Tighten by moving entries up (e.g. medium=approve) or loosen for dev (e.g. high=allow — not recommended).
